response:
Convert the tax liability for the art collection by applying the 20% tax rate to its appraised value of $2 million. Convert the 5% discount to reduce the taxable amount by 5% of the original tax rate. Convert the tax on the art collection to $280,000 after applying the discount.

Convert the real estate portfolio's value by increasing it by 10% to account for the misvaluation error. Convert the new value to $5.5 million. Convert the tax calculation by applying the progressive tax rate: 10% on the first $1 million, 15% on the next $2 million, and 20% on the remaining $2.5 million. Convert the total tax on the real estate portfolio to $1,125,000.

Convert the securities portfolio by applying the 15% tax rate to its value of $3 million. Convert the $100,000 deduction to reduce the taxable amount. Convert the tax on the securities portfolio to $345,000 after the deduction.

Convert the total tax liability by summing the taxes from all three assets. Convert the final total tax liability for the estate to $1,750,000. Convert the total tax liability for the estate with the corrected real estate value to $2,150,000.
